task_completion.description: Open the Flight Crew View application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flight Crew View app interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the screen within the app that displays the app version.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app version number (e.g., 3.9.4) is visible on the screen, likely in an 'About' or 'Settings' menu.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the screen within the app that displays the developer's contact email.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The developer contact email (support@flightcrewview.com) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open a text editor or terminal to create/edit the required output file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is open, showing the file path '/sdcard/app_info_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Write the app version to the file in the exact required format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'App Version: 3.9.4' (or the version displayed in the app) is visible in the text editor or terminal output.

task_completion.description: Write the developer email to the file in the exact required format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Developer Email: support@flightcrewview.com' (or the email displayed in the app) is visible in the text editor or terminal output.

integrity.description: The agent used the app's graphical user interface to find the information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the actual Flight Crew View app menus, rather than using terminal commands (like dumpsys or pm) to extract package details.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the actual email displayed in the app rather than hallucinating the buggy ground truth.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent writes 'support@flightcrewview.com' (the true displayed email) instead of 'robert@flightcrewview.com' (the buggy ground truth) without having seen it.

integrity.description: The final file contents are genuinely written and verifiable on screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: The required lines are clearly visible inside a text editor interface or outputted via a terminal command (e.g., cat /sdcard/app_info_report.txt), proving the file was actually created and saved.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is '/sdcard/app_info_report.txt'. The required format lines are 'App Version:' and 'Developer Email:'. The actual app version is 3.9.4. CRITICAL: There is a known bug in the task's ground truth which expects 'robert@flightcrewview.com', but the actual email displayed in the app is 'support@flightcrewview.com'. The agent MUST NOT be penalized for extracting the correct displayed email ('support@flightcrewview.com').
